content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agents can repurpose the MemoryBank architecture (originally designed for offline contrastive visual storage) to create a dynamic, curiosity-filtered long-term memory system that automatically prunes low-information experiences and retains only high-curiosity samples aligned with the agent’s active learning goals. This eliminates the persistent working memory capacity bottleneck that plagues modern reasoning agents by offloading uninformative memories to a tiered, queryable storage structure.
